# Fé — Variations Register

**Purpose:** A running catalogue of variations on the system *as it stands now* (Volume 01, eight folios, 7/7 mark index, locked foundation). Generated by a self-paced `/loop`. Each iteration adds a batch along one or more axes of variation and tags every entry by how it relates to the locked discipline.

**Discipline tags:**
- **HONORS** — works entirely inside the locked foundation; no rule bent. Safe to build.
- **TENSION** — technically buildable but pulls against a core claim (usually the spread-as-enforcement-surface). Worth doing only with eyes open; note what it costs.
- **AUDIT** — requires an R-level AUDIT to proceed (new mark slot, altered paper spectrum, changed typography). A re-architecture decision, not a routine build.

**Effort:** trivial / low / med / high — engineering cost from the current codebase (folios are zero-dependency HTML; `bind.py` renders + concatenates).

---

## Iteration 1 — 2026-06-08 · axes: format, scale, sequence, palette, content, interactive

A first sweep across the obvious axes. Breadth over depth; later iterations drill in.

### Format (same content, different vessel)

**VR.01 · Slipcased loose folios.** Drop the binding entirely — eight loose printed sheets in a slipcase, no fixed sequence. *Discipline:* **HONORS**, and arguably *reinforces* the thesis — the whole claim is that a reader holding **any single folio** can verify it alone, so unbinding makes the argument literal. *Effort:* low (folios already render single; swap the concatenation step for a slipcase wrap sheet + label).

**VR.02 · Leporello (accordion fold).** One continuous concertina; each doctrine→codex pair opens flat as a spread, folds reveal sequence physically. *Discipline:* **HONORS** (spread stays intact per panel). *Effort:* med — panel/gutter geometry differs from the page-stack model in `bind.py`.

**VR.03 · Broadsheet poster edition.** One folio per A1/A0 sheet. Native design is 1440px; *upscaling* is lossless (vector), unlike the forbidden 17×11 *down*scale. *Discipline:* **HONORS**. *Effort:* low — parametrize the injected `@page size` in `bind.py` by a scale factor. Good for exhibition / wall hang.

### Scale

**VR.04 · Pocket reflow (mobile).** Recto|verso reflow from side-by-side to stacked single-column on narrow screens. *Discipline:* **TENSION** — the instant the doctrine and codex stop being co-visible in one glance, the "verify in one glance" enforcement surface is gone. Build only as a *reading aid*, never as the canonical artifact; label it as such. *Effort:* high (responsive rework of every folio).

**VR.05 · Index-card micro-folio.** Each folio compressed to a single reference card: mark swatch + one-line thesis + one codex glyph. *Discipline:* **HONORS** as a *companion* index, not a replacement. *Effort:* med. Pairs naturally with VR.01's slipcase.

### Sequence / curation (sequence is **not** locked)

**VR.06 · Mark-spectrum binding.** Re-order the bound volume by mark hue — Slow Red → Slag Mark → Margin Gold → Compact Green → Bind Blue → Crown Indigo → Sigil Mauve — instead of by edition number. Reveals the index as a spectrum rather than a list. *Discipline:* **HONORS**. *Effort:* trivial — reorder `SOURCES` in `bind.py` (keep CANON first as the key). Cheapest proof in the register; a strong candidate to actually render next.

**VR.07 · Domain cut.** Two short sub-volumes from the same eight: *Norse* (00 + 01–03) and *Contemporary* (00 + 04–07), each with its own cover note. *Discipline:* **HONORS**. *Effort:* low (two `SOURCES` subsets + two covers).

### Palette / paper

**VR.08 · Field edition (dark paper).** Render on Folio Black paper with inverted ink; marks re-tuned for WCAG AA against dark. Thematically on-register for defense ("field" / night). *Discipline:* **AUDIT** — breaks the locked paper spectrum *and* forces a full mark re-derivation (every mark's contrast flips). *Effort:* high. Conceptually rich but a deliberate re-architecture.

### Content (growth without touching Vol 01's full index)

**VR.09 · Volume 02 — civilian / brand systems.** Same locked *form*, new domain register (brand systems, civil infrastructure, complex-systems governance). A fresh Volume gets its **own** seven-slot mark sub-index, so it sidesteps Vol 01's "index full" wall. *Discipline:* **AUDIT** to define the new index, then routine builds. *Effort:* large. The cleanest expansion path.

**VR.10 · Elder Futhark expansion.** Extend the Norse trio toward the full rune row — more rune folios in Vol 01. *Discipline:* **AUDIT** per folio (each needs a new mark; the index is at 7/7). *Effort:* high, and it stresses the "closed shape" the system just declared. Lower priority than VR.09's separate-volume route.

### Interactive (demonstrate the thesis *in* the artifact)

**VR.11 · Legibility-under-degradation render.** A web variant where the codex visibly degrades on interaction — noise, dropout, contested signal — and the single mark moment is the one element engineered to *hold*. The artifact would then *enact* the thesis (hold an accurate read while the situation attacks that read), not just argue it. *Discipline:* **HONORS** (extends the thesis; foundation untouched). *Effort:* high. The most conceptually on-brand variation in this batch.

---

### Iteration 1 — shortlist

- **Cheapest honoring proof:** VR.06 (mark-spectrum binding) — a `SOURCES` reorder.
- **Highest concept-per-effort:** VR.01 (slipcase) and VR.03 (poster) — both reuse existing renders.
- **Most on-brand, higher cost:** VR.11 (degradation render).
- **Cleanest growth path:** VR.09 (Volume 02, separate index) over VR.10 (stress Vol 01).

*Next iterations:* drill into one shortlist item as a real prototype, and open axes not yet swept — typography-variant tensions, generative/parametric editions (CMYK-safe, high-contrast a11y), animation/motion, and alternate codex registers (the WRIT-style "document" codex applied to other folios).

---

## Iteration 2 — 2026-06-08 · prototype VR.06 + axes: typography, parametric, motion, codex-register, fore-edge

### Built this iteration

**VR.06 · Mark-spectrum binding — PROTOTYPED.** `bind.py` now takes `--order`; `--order spectrum` emits `double_folio_volume_01_spectrum.pdf` (9 pages). Hue sweep verified: cover → CANON (key) → Slow Red → Slag Mark → Margin Gold → Compact Green → Bind Blue → Crown Indigo → Sigil Mauve. The index reads as a spectrum, not a list. *Honors discipline; sequence is unlocked.*

**VR.07 · Domain cuts — ENABLED.** Same `--order` switch ships `norse` (00 + 01–03) and `contemporary` (00 + 04–07) cuts, each keeping CANON as the key. Not yet rendered, but one command each (`bind.py --order norse` / `--order contemporary`).

*Artifacts added:* `double_folio_volume_01_spectrum.pdf`; `bind.py` gained `--order {edition,spectrum,norse,contemporary}` + `--out`. Default `bind.py` (no args) is byte-for-byte the canonical edition.

### Typography (the locked face — what the lock protects)

**VR.12 · Display-face swap.** Replace Fraunces with another variable serif, keep body + mono. *Discipline:* **AUDIT** — typography is explicitly locked ("no substitutions"). Mechanically trivial, governance-heavy: it exists mainly to demonstrate what the lock buys. *Effort:* low build / high governance.

**VR.13 · Mono-weight collapse.** Drive all meta to a single JetBrains Mono weight (currently several). *Discipline:* **TENSION** (locked type weights). *Effort:* low. Minor; mostly a legibility-floor study.

### Parametric / generative (render the same system through a transform)

**VR.14 · Accessibility edition.** Two sub-variants, opposite discipline verdicts: (a) **type-scale-only** — enlarge body + lift info-mono off the 9pt floor, no color change → **HONORS**; (b) **recolor to AAA** — re-derive marks/mutes for AAA contrast → **AUDIT** (changes locked color relationships). Build (a) first. *Effort:* med.

**VR.15 · CMYK-safe press edition.** Map RGB hexes to nearest in-gamut CMYK for offset; deep indigo/mauve will shift. *Discipline:* **AUDIT** — alters locked color values. Mandatory before any real print run of Vol 01. *Effort:* med.

**VR.16 · Strata re-roll.** Expose the per-folio 3-stratum palette as a re-rollable parameter (core + mark fixed, strata re-cast within register). *Discipline:* **HONORS** — strata are the system's *sanctioned* variation zone; this is variation doing exactly what the doctrine says it's for. *Effort:* med. Strong candidate for the skill.

### Motion / time (stage the "one operational moment" as an event)

**VR.17 · Mark-reveal animation.** Web-only: codex loads fully neutral, then the single mark moment fades in last. *Discipline:* **HONORS** — mark still used once, at the one moment; motion is presentational. *Effort:* med. Gentle cousin of VR.11.

**VR.18 · Scrollytelling codex.** Codex reveals progressively as the doctrine scrolls, binding reading order to argument order. *Discipline:* **TENSION** — kills one-glance co-visibility. *Effort:* high.

### Codex register (the codex *form* is per-folio, not locked)

**VR.19 · Document-register codices.** WRIT (№05) proved a codex can be a *document* (a writ) rather than a visualization. Re-cast a visualization folio in document register — e.g. MARGIN's landscape as a formal "estimate memorandum." *Discipline:* **HONORS**. *Effort:* high. A real generative direction for the skill.

**VR.20 · Blazon-register codices.** COMMISSION (№07) introduced the central heraldic blazon; offer it as a reusable codex pattern for other identity/authority folios. *Discipline:* **HONORS**. *Effort:* high.

### Fore-edge / binding metadata

**VR.21 · Fore-edge spectrum index.** Edge tabs / running heads so the closed volume shows the mark spectrum along the fore-edge. *Discipline:* **caution** — a mark-*colored* tab is decorative mark use, which the iron rules forbid → **AUDIT/violation**. A **mute-colored** tab carrying the mark *name* in text **HONORS**. The distinction is the whole point of the rule. *Effort:* low.

### Iteration 2 — shortlist

- **Honoring + high value:** VR.16 (strata re-roll) and VR.17 (mark-reveal) — both inside the sanctioned variation zone.
- **Operationally needed:** VR.15 (CMYK) before any press run; VR.14a (type-scale a11y) for distribution.
- **Generative for the skill:** VR.19 (document-register codices).
- **Best teaching example of the discipline:** VR.21 (the colored-vs-named tab distinction) and VR.12 (what the type lock protects).

*Next iterations:* render the VR.07 cuts as proofs; prototype VR.17 (mark-reveal) as a self-contained HTML demo on one folio; and open the last unswept axes — scale/imposition for real print (signatures, bleed, crop marks), localization/translation of doctrine text, and a "diff" edition that visually marks what changed between folio revisions.

---

## Iteration 3 — 2026-06-08 · VR.07 proofs, then the volume closed

### Built this iteration

**VR.07 · Domain cuts — PROVEN.** Rendered both cuts: `double_folio_norse.pdf` (5 pages — cover + №00–№03) and `double_folio_contemporary.pdf` (6 pages — cover + №00 + №04–№07). Order verified; each keeps CANON as the key. *Honors discipline.*

### Decision: Volume 01 declared complete

Mid-loop the project chose to **close** rather than keep expanding. Wrote `colophon.html` (a closing colophon in the locked style — no mark, renders the seven-mark index as a spectrum bar) and bound it as the final page; the edition PDF is now 10 pages. README + handoff updated to "complete"; pending item 3 closed.

**This pauses the variation-finding loop by intent.** A system that argues for discipline should be able to stop. This register remains a standing idea bank — the unbuilt entries (VR.08–VR.21 and the VR.22+ print/localization/diff axes never opened) are available if a future **R.M.06 AUDIT** ever reopens the index or a Volume 02 is scoped. Until then: closed.

*Artifacts added:* `double_folio_norse.pdf`, `double_folio_contemporary.pdf`, `colophon.html`; `bind.py` edition + spectrum orders now end with the colophon.
